sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.104 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: For a group word$w(x_1, , x_n)$on$n$letters, define$e_{0}(x_1, , x_n) = x_1$and$e_{k+1}(x_1, , x_n) = w(e_k(x_1, , x_n), , x_n)$for all$k$. A group$G$is said to satisfy the if for all$x_1, , x_n G$there exists$m$such that$e_m(x_1, , x_n)=1$. Conjecture: For every non-trivial word$w$, if a finitely generated branch group$G$(see 15.12) satisfies the iterated identity$w$, then$G$is a torsion group. T...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
